D3 Chemistry L5 Evaluate Reference

Charge-Transfer Bands — MLCT vs LMCT

an electron leaps between metal and ligand orbitals — direction depends on which side has the lower-energy LUMO; the intense colours of permanganate, Prussian blue, and Ru-bipyridyl all come from CT

A charge-transfer (CT) transition moves an electron between metal-centred and ligand-centred orbitals. LMCT (ligand → metal) is favoured when the metal is in a high oxidation state (low-energy d orbitals): KMnO₄ Mn(VII), [CrO₄]²⁻ Cr(VI). MLCT (metal → ligand) is favoured by low-oxidation metals with π-acceptor ligands: [Ru(bipy)₃]²⁺, [Fe(bipy)₃]²⁺. CT bands are typically 100× more intense than d-d bands — hence the deep colour.

Toggle the direction. The arrow flips between metal-derived and ligand-derived orbital levels; example compounds + their measured λ_max are listed. The colour swatch is the complement of the absorption band.
